title: AphidMASH (Aphid Multi-Allele Scanning Haplotags)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"b939u","text":"This protocol describes a multiplex-PCR amplicon sequencing approach designed to facilitate the genotyping of Sitobion avenae, the identification of the L1014F mutation, the screening of regions harbouring additional mutations known to infer insecticide resistance in other species, and the detection of yellow dwarf virus in aphids.  The design of aphid targets was facilitated by re-sequencing data from 12 S. avenae clonal lineages (BioProject: PRJNA1039626 ) and the design of virus targets using sequence data from a HTS Survey (1). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":110,"length":15},{"style":"italic","offset":409,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5k68k","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e5tid","text":"This methodology is based on the original GT-seq protocol described by Campbell (2) and adapted from methods described in https://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.e6nvw53zdvmk/v2 and (3).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":122,"length":65}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":122,"length":56}],"data":{}},{"key":"52s1l","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fidvt","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"899d2","text":"1. S. Byrne, M. Schughart and V. Ballandras et al. The first survey using high-throughput sequencing of cereal and barley yellow dwarf viruses in Irish spring and winter barley crops. Irish Journal of Agricultural and Food Research. 2024. Vol. 63(1):1-16. steps: Prepare the aphids by removing excess ethanol. This can be achieved by removing the aphids from the sample and placing them on absorbent tissue to air-dry for 15 seconds.
Place the rack(s) in the the arms of a MM400 mixer mill and set the agitation for 00:30:00 at 24 Hz.

Thaw the RNA on ice and briefly centrifuge the plate to collect contents in bottom of wells. Transfer 2 µL from each well into a new nuclease free 96 well plate. Add 1 µL of Random Hexamer Primer (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat: SO142), and 9.5 µL of double distilled water (ddH 2 O). Seal the plate with using a foil adhesive sheet and incubate the plate at 65 °C, for 5 minutes .

PCR1 is performed with the cDNA from above as a template. This first PCR is designed to amplify the targets in the aphid and yellow dwarf virus. PCR1 primers: A B C 1 Target ID Forward primer (5’to 3’) Reverse primer (5’to 3’) 2 AM_01 GGGACTGTTTACACGTTGGAGA TCAGCCGTAGGCACTGATAAA 3 AM_02 GGGACTGTTTACACGTTGGAGA GCCCTCCTAAATTCTTGCACTG 4 AM_03 CTTCGAGTATTTAAGTTGGCAAAATC TTTCCAAATAACTGCATACCCATAAC 5 AM_04 CACCACCACCACCACTTC GGTCACCTCTGCTACGCC 6 AM_05 GCGATCACGTCCAACATGTAG GCGAAGGCGTCATCAATAATG 7 AM_06 CGGATCGCCACTAATCAAGAG GTGGTCGACGTGGTCATTGT 8 AM_07 GCATTAGACACATACCGGAAAATTC ACAATTGGACTCCGTATAAAGTTGA 9 AM_08 GAAGCAAGCATGAATCTAACAAGTG GTTAGTCTCTGGTGTTCTCGC 10 AM_09 GTGGCTGTTTTGGATACTTTGC TTTCCTTCACCGATGTAGCCG 11 AM_10 CATTGAGACTCAGGTTGATTCAAGC TTGAAATTAGGGTGACTGATAGAAGC 12 AM_11 GTAACGGCTGTTCTGACTGG CAAAGACAGTTTGGGTTTACCG 13 AM_12 TGCCATCTCATCTTCCCTTTGTT GTATGGGATAAGAAAATGGCCAAAG 14 AM_13 ACGAAATGTCTTGGAATAGCCTTAG GTGGGTTGGCATGCAAAGTG 15 AM_16 CAGCAGCACTTCTGAATGGC CCTTGCAGGCCAAGTTGTG 16 AM_18 GCGCCTCAATCCATCACAGA CAGGGCACTTCCAATGTTCTC 17 AM_19 CGTGACCATTATTTGACGTGTAC CCGAAATGTAACTGTGACGGTG 18 AM_21 CTCCCCACCAATCAGGACTAC GGATTCATTTGATAGCCCATCTTG 19 AM_22 GCATTTTGATCGGTTCTTTGTCGA GATAACGAATCCCAAAGCTGGAC 20 AM_23 GGACTAACAGCAAGTGAAGGC GCCCTACATCGCCATCTTATAG 21 AM_24 CAGGCAGACGCACAACTCAG AGCTGATACCGGGCCAGAAA 22 AM_25 GGTTGGAGAAGGAAACGGGG AGTTGACGGTGGTTGTGACG 23 AM_26 TCGGGTTGCTGTTCCTCGT ATGATGATCGACCTGCGCC 24 AM_27 TTGAGCGGAAATCACGGGAC GCCAACACTGGTCACAGAC 25 AM_28 CCTGTACTCTTCCTTCAACCTTTG CGATGAGGCGCACAATATTGAC 26 AM_29 TCTTGATGACGTTCATCCCGTG AGATCTGAGTATACTGATGACGTTTC 27 AM_30 GGAGTTGAACAAACAGTTGCTC AAATCTCCATGGGAGGCAGC 28 AM_31 AAAGAACCCGTGGTCGTGC CTGCACGTCGTTTCGTTGAAG 29 AM_32 CCACCAGAACCACCTTCAATAGA CCGGTGGAGGTGACAGTAGTT 30 AM_33 ATGTTGTTGTTGCAGGCCG TGTTTCTCACGGTTCCCGAC 31 AM_34 GACATGCTGCACTGTGTTTGG GTGGAGACCTGGTATAGATTGC 32 AM_35 GAGGTAGTCGTTGATGTCGC GTTCGGGTGGCAGAACTTTC 33 AM_36 CAGATGAATACGACAAAGTGTTTGG GCTGACACCTCAAGATCACAC 34 AM_37 CAGCAATTGTAGCCAATAAACGG GGCTGTCATTTCCATCTTCAATTTG 35 AM_38 GGTACCGATAACGTTAGCCAC GGAAATCCCATCACGTGACAC 36 AM_39 TGAACGCATGCACAAACAGT CGCTAGTAGTTTCTCTGAGTGC 37 AM_41 AATTTGGATTCCACATTGTAGCACC TAGGTTTAGGCATCCTCGCC 38 AM_43 GAGGTTCGGGTAATTGGTGTG CTACATAGACTGCCTGTGCCTG 39 AM_44 TAGACACTCGCGTACGTACC GCTGATATTCCCGCCACTGT 40 AM_45 GAGCTTTGCACATGGTGTTGAT CAGAAACAGGGACATCATCTGC 41 AM_46 TTACGACGCTGTTTGCCG AACCGTCGCTGAATCACC 42 AM_47 TCTAACGGTTGACAGGAGGAC CGGTAAATGACTTGTTGCACAGG 43 AM_48 TATGCCCGTCACGTACAGATC AAGTGGTACGTGAGCCGC 44 AM_51 GGATCCCCCACTGTGCTTTC CCCGTGTTCCCACTTAAGCG 45 AM_52 AGGTGAGAGGCCACAGAATG GCTTCTCATTGGCTCTGTCTGT 46 AM_53 CGACGTCAAAAGTTACATGTCGG CATCTGTTCCATACGAAATGACG 47 AM_54 CAAGAGGAGGAGGGGCAAAT CTTGAGTATTCCACCTGAAAGCG 48 AM_55 AATTGGCTGGTTCTGGGAGAC GCCACCTCCGCTCTCATTT 49 AM_56 CTCTATTAGGACTGCCGGAGAT AACACTCTTCTCTCCACTGCAAC 50 AM_58 GCATCTCTGTCAGCAAACTTGG CCCGTGTTCCCACTTAAGCG 51 AM_59 GTGCGAGCGGCCAAGAAATA GCTTCTCATTGGCTCTGTCTGT 52 AM_61 GGAGACGACTGTGTCATCATCA CATTAATAGAGACCGGTTTTGACTG 53 AM_62 CAATCGAGCAGGACCCAGAC GCATTGCGATAGACTGGGGC 54 AM_63 ATTGACTTCCTTGAAGCCATCAA GTAGGCGGTCAAAGGTAAGC 55 AM_64 GGATTATCCACCGAGGACCAAG TCCAATCGGAGACTGACCAGT 56 AM_65 CAGCCAGTGGTTGTGGTC GATACTTCCAGAGGAGTTGCC 57 AM_66 TCTTACAGTTCGTCAGCGAGG ATCATCTTCGCTGGGAAGCT 58 AM_67 TCGACAGTTCCTCACGATTCTT GCAACGACCGAGCAGACAA 59 AM_68 AGCACAACCTTAGGGAAGCG GACGCTTCCGCATCCGAA 60 AM_69 GCTGTCTGGTTGCAAGGCAT GCCACAATGATAGGCAGCCA 61 AM_70 GCAGTTCCGATGTTTGTCTTTCC AGCCCCAATATTCCACCCTTC 62 AM_71 GCTGACAACAGTTGGAAGTACG GAAGTTTTCTGGACAGGCGGA PCR 1 primers pairs designed to target aphid’s and viruses' genomes. These primers were retained after verifying that they targeted unique regions in the genome and that no primer–primer interactions would occur during PCR. The targets AM_01 AM_02 and AM_03 were designed to amplify the regions of theVGSC including kdr and super- kdr mutations. The targets AM_04 to AM_48 were designed to amplify regions of the aphids' genome to analyse their genetic diversity. The targets AM_51 to AM_62 were designed to amplify regions of BYDV's genome. The targets AM_63 to AM_71 were designed to amplify regions of BYDV's genome. All PCR1 primers are composed of the sequence matching to the organism’s genome (aphid or virus) and Illumina sequencing primer tags attached to the 5’ end of the forward primer and the 5’ end of the reverse complement of the reverse primer.These sequences, R1: 5’ CACCACCACCACCACTTC 3’ and R2: 5’ CCATGGAGACGATGCGG 3’,for the forward primer and the reverse primer respectively, served as anchorage regions for the PCR2 primers.

Prepare a dilution of the PCR1 product by pipetting one volume of the PCR1 product into the respective wells of a new nuclease free 96 well plate. Add 14 volumes of ddH 2 O to each well. Seal with an aluminium sticker after mixing by pipetting.

Reaction Mix preparation in each of the 96 well: 12.5 µL, Multiplex PCR master Mix 2 µL of the primer pool solution at 1 micromolar (µM) , each primer 9.5 µL of ddH 2 O 1 µL of template cDNA to the respective well Tip: Prepare a large volume of reaction mix in a tube (corresponding to 100 - 104 wells), without the template cDNA. Distribute the reaction mix in the wells using a multichannel pipette. Add the template cDNA using the multichannel pipette afterward. this saves time and reduce the risks of cross-contamination. If out of the freezer from long time storage, taw the cDNA plate on ice in the lab and briefly centrifugate the plate in a bench plate centrifuge if available. Seal the plate with an aluminium sticker. Briefly centrifugate the plate in the bench bench plate centrifuge.
PCR2 primers : PCR2 primers are composed of an Illumina sequencing adaptor, a 6nt barcode used to index the position of each well into each plate, the sequencing primer tag:
PCR2 product normalisation: This step is performed using the NGS Normalization 96-Well Kit:
Insert each aphid into an individual tube of the Lysing matrix Z 96 tube rack (be aware of static).

Add 300 µL, Quick-RNA lysing buffer from the Quick-RNA 96 kit (Zymo) into each tube and seal the tubes with lids provided and further cover with an aluminium foil sheet. Lids are fragile and can easily detach from each other, make sure that each tube is securely sealed to avoid leaks of the lysing buffer during agitation.

Position each aphid in an individual well of a new 96 well plate pre-filled with molecular-grade water for a few seconds to wash off any remaining ethanol, and then place each aphid back on an absorbent tissue for 00:00:30 to dry.
After removing the rack(s) from the mixer mill, centrifuge 1000 x g, °C, 2 minutes and place the plate on ice.

Take 200 µL of the lysate supernatant from each tube and transfer to the corresponding well of a RNAse free 96 deep-well plate. Add 200 µL of 99% ethanol to each well. Mix well by pipetting up and down.

Transfer the mixture to the corresponding wells of a Silicon A‱ Plate, mounted on a Collection Plate and centrifuge 2500 x g, 18-25 °C, 5 minutes . Discard the flow-through and replace the Silicon A plate on the Collection plate.

Add 400 µL, / well of RNA Prep Buffer to the plate and centrifuge 2500 x g, 18-25 °C, 5 minutes . Discard the flow-through and replace the Silicon A plate on the Collection plate. The manufacturer's protocol includes an optional DNase I treatment prior to this step. This has not been used in this protocol.
Add 500 µL, / well of RNA Wash Buffer to the plate and centrifuge 2500 x g, 18-25 °C, 5 minutes . Discard the flow-through and repeat this step.
Mount the Silicon A plate onto an Elution Plate and add 25 µL DNase/RNase-Free Water directly to the matrix and centrifuge 2500 x g, 18-25 °C, 5 minutes .
The RNA can then be stored at -70 °C or you can proceed directly to cDNA synthesis.

Add 4 µL of RevertAid reaction buffer , 0.5 µL of RiboLock RNase , 2 µL of dNTP Mix ( 10 millimolar (mM) each) and 1 µL of RevertAid reverse transcriptase (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Waltham, Massachusetts, USA; Cat: K1691) to each well for a final reaction volume of 20 µL . Seal the plate with using a foil adhesive sheet.

Briefly centrifuge the plate to collect contents in bottom of wells, and place the plate into a thermal cycler and incubate for 00:10:00 at 25 °C , followed by an incubation at 42 °C for 01:00:00 and 70 °C for 00:10:00
The cDNA plates can then be stored at -20 °C for short term storage or -70 °C for longer term storage.

Prepare the primer mix dilution: Put one volume of each primer (forward and reverse), delivered at concentration 500 micromolar (µM) , into a tube and complete the dilution with three volumes of ddH 2 O and mix thoroughly by pipetting. This brings the primer mix's concentration to 4 micromolar (µM) for each primer. Take a fraction of this solution and dilute it into three volume of ddH 2 O equivalent to the volume of this fraction to bring the final concentration of the primer mix to 1 micromolar (µM) , for each primer.

Thermo-Cycling: Place the PCR plate in a MiniAmp Plus Thermal Cycler (Applied Biosystems , A37835) and run the following PCR cycle: 95 °C 00:05:00 followed 30 cycles of : 95 °C 00:00:30 a ramp down temperature decrease 2 °C / second until reaching 60 °C 72 °C 00:02:00 At the end of the cycles, keep the plate at 10 °C . Remove the PCR plate from the thermal cycler, let it cool down on ice and spin it in the bench bench plate centrifuge for one or two minutes. At this stage, the PCR 1 product can be stored at -70 °C for long time storage, -20 °C for short time storage or directly used for PCR2 Cross contamination can occur until the end of PCR2, do not pool or mix the well's content before the end of PCR2. Opening the plate sealing without centrifugating or too abruptly can lead to cross contamination. If cross-contamination is induced during PCR1, restart PCR1, do not proceed to PCR2 with a low quality PCR1 PCR 1 quality can be assessed with a Nanodrop 2000C (Thermo Scientific, TSC-ND2000C) and / or a migration of the PCR product in an agarose gel, expecting an amplicon's length of about 150 bp

Prepare a dilution of the PCR2 primers to 10 micromolar (µM) : PCR2 primers were delivered at concentration 100 micromolar (µM) . In a new nuclease-free 96 well plate, mix 1 volume of each primer (forward) and 9 volume of ddH 2 O in the respective primer's well. In a new nuclease-free tube, mix 1 volume of one of the reverse primer and 9 volume of ddH 2 O. Do not mix PCR 2 primers that have different I5 or I7 indexes
Reverse primer I7 indexes: A B 1 Plate ID Index sequence 5’-3' 2 Plate 1 ATCACG 3 Plate 2 CGATGT 4 Plate 3 TTAGGC 5 Plate 4 TGACCA 6 Plate 5 ACAGTG 7 Plate 6 GCCAAT

Forward primer I5 indexes plate layout: A 1 A1_ATCACG 2 B1_CGATGT 3 C1_TTAGGC 4 D1_TGACCA 5 E1_ACAGTG 6 F1_GCCAAT 7 G1_CAGATC 8 H1_ACTTGA 9 A2_GATCAG 10 B2_TAGCTT 11 C2_GGCTAC 12 D2_CTTGTA 13 E2_AGTCAA 14 F2_AGTTCC 15 G2_ATGTCA 16 H2_CCGTCC 17 A3_GTAGAG 18 B3_GTCCGC 19 C3_GTGAAA 20 D3_GTGGCC 21 E3_GTTTCG 22 F3_CGTACG 23 G3_GAGTGG 24 H3_GGTAGC 25 A4_ACTGAT 26 B4_ATGAGC 27 C4_ATTCCT 28 D4_CAAAAG 29 E4_CAACTA 30 F4_CACCGG 31 G4_CACGAT 32 H4_CACTCA 33 A5_CAGGCG 34 B5_CATGGC 35 C5_CATTTT 36 D5_CCAACA 37 E5_CGGAAT 38 F5_CTAGCT 39 G5_CTATAC 40 H5_CTCAGA 41 A6_GACGAC 42 B6_TAATCG 43 C6_TACAGC 44 D6_TATAAT 45 E6_TCATTC 46 F6_TCCCGA 47 G6_TCGAAG 48 H6_TCGGCA 49 A7_AAGTAC 50 B7_TGCCAT 51 C7_CTCCTT 52 D7_AGATAG 53 E7_GATATA 54 F7_ATAATA 55 G7_GGTGCT 56 H7_TGTGGA 57 A8_CGAGAA 58 B8_GCAGGA 59 C8_GCCGCG 60 D8_AGCGCA 61 E8_TCCGTT 62 F8_ATCGTC 63 G8_CCGCAA 64 H8_AACTTG 65 A9_AAGGTA 66 B9_GCGCTC 67 C9_CCTCGT 68 D9_ACGATT 69 E9_AGACTC 70 F9_TGGTCT 71 G9_GGAATT 72 H9_ACATCT 73 A10_TTCTCC 74 B10_ATCTAT 75 C10_TGCATA 76 D10_AATAGG 77 E10_CGGTTA 78 F10_GAGACT 79 G10_GCTCCA 80 H10_ATGCAG 81 A11_GGCCTG 82 B11_TTACTG 83 C11_TCAAGT 84 D11_AGCAGT 85 E11_CCTTAG 86 F11_TTGGAT 87 G11_TCTCAC 88 H11_GTCTTA 89 A12_CTAACG 90 B12_ATACAC 91 C12_GCATTC 92 D12_TACTCT 93 E12_TGGACT 94 F12_CCATAG 95 G12_TAAGGT 96 H12_TTGGCG

PCR mix in each well of a new nuclease free 96 well PCR plate for a final volume of 25 µL per well: 12 µL of Multiplex PCR Master Mix 8 µL of ddH 2 O 1 µL of Forward primer 10 micromolar (µM) 1 µL of Reverse primer 10 micromolar (µM) 3 µL of the diluted PCR1 product

Thermo-Cycling: Place the PCR plate in a MiniAmp Plus Thermal Cycler (Applied Biosystems , A37835) and run the following PCR cycle: 95 °C for 00:15:00 10 cycles of: 98 °C for 00:00:10 65 °C for 00:00:30 72 °C for 00:00:30 at the end of the cycles: hold at 72 °C for 00:05:00 and keep at 10 °C Remove the plate from the thermal cycler and centrifugate in the bench plate centrifuge and keep on ice if used directly for the next steps. PCR2 products can be stored at -20 °C for short time storage and -80 °C for long time storage.
Prepare a working concentration of Wash Solution A by adding 90 mL of 96 - 100% ethanol
Add 100 µL of Elution Buffer B to each well of the 96-Well Plate.

Repeat step 26.4 for a second wash and Centrifuge the assembly at maximum speed or 3200 x g, °C, 15 minutes . Gently pat the bottom of the 96-Well Normalization Plate on paper towels. Stack the 96-Well Normalization Plate and the 96-Well Elution Plate (provided).
Add 75 µL of Buffer SK directly to each well of the PCR2 plate, Mix by pipetting up and down and centrifuge the plate.

Apply 400 µL of Wash Solution A to each well of the 96-Well Normalization Plate.Centrifuge the assembly at maximum speed or 3200 x g, °C, 2 minutes . Ensure that all of the lysate from each well has passed through into the bottom plate. If the entire lysate volume has not passed, centrifuge for an additional 2 minutes. Discard the flowthrough . Reassemble the 96-Well Normalization Plate and the 96-WellCollection Plate

Place the 96-Well Normalization Plate on top of a provided 96-Well Collection Plate and add the mixture from the PCR2 plate into the wells of the 96-Well Normalization Plate. Centrifuge the assembly at maximum speed or 3200 x g, °C, 2 minutes . Discard the flowthrough. Reassemble the 96-Well Normalization Plate and the 96-Well Collection Plate Ensure that all of the lysate from each well has passed through into the bottom plate. If the entire lysate volume has not passed, centrifuge for an additional 2 minutes.
Centrifuge the assembly at 200 x g, °C, 1 minute followed by 3200 x g, °C, 4 minutes
Pooling libraries, Concentration - Purification: At this stage,PCR2 products are barcoded and normalized. Pool the normalized products in a single tube.
The PCR product purification is performed using the QIAquick PCR Purification Kit and a bench centrifuge. Before starting, add ethanol (96–100%) to Buffer PE before use (see bottle label for volume).

Place QIAquick column in a clean 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube. To elute DNA, add 50 µL of Buffer EB (10 mM Tris·Cl, pH 8.5) or nuclease free water (pH 7.0–8.5) to the center of the QIAquick membrane and centrifuge the column for 17900 x g, °C, 2 minutes .

To wash, add 700 µL of Buffer PE to the QIAquick column and centrifuge 17900 x g, °C, 1 minute . Discard flow-through and place the QIAquick column back into the same tube. Centrifuge 17900 x g, °C, 1 minute . The residual ethanol from the Buffer PE must be dried.

Prepare collection tubes with a QIAquick Spin column inserted in each tube. Ideally, prepare 4 of them as important volumes of libraries will be purified. While 2 tube-column assemblies will be spinning, the two other ones can be filled with the solution to purify. This will be repeated multiple times, until the totality of mix of PCR2 product with Buffer PB passed through. Processing one library (corresponding to one 96 well plate) at the time is recommended. in this case, it is not necessary to label the columns or the collection tubes as they contain the same solution. If multiple libraries are processed at the same time, always label correctly all the tubes and columns to not mix the libraries. Proceed to the filtering of the mix of PCR2 product with Buffer PB to bind DNA by applying the mix to the QIAquick column and centrifuge 17900 x g, °C, 1 minute . Repeat as many times as necessary with the same set of columns to filter all the mix. Discard the flowthrough each time and re-incert the QIAquick Spin column in its collection tube. Do not put more than 600 - 650 µL at the time otherwise the flowthrough will touch the bottom of the column.
Sub-sample a volume of the PCR2 pooled product mix and add 5 volumes of Buffer PB into a new nuclease-free falcon tube.

Size selection: As mentioned in the PotatoMASH library construction protocol: PotatoMASH library construction by Maria de la O MOLP Leyva Perez "This step is meant to remove PCR1 primer-dimer amplified products in PCR2 and other unwanted secondary products from the libraries. All core primers are designed to produce libraries of around 300bp. Therefore, electrophoresis in agarose gel is a low-cost and easy way to separate them."

Make a 2% agarose gel and pour the totality of the purified library from step 27.5 in the "wide wells" formed in the gel. "Wide wells can be achieved, for example, by covering three 7 mm. wide x 1mm thick comb teeth (3 wells) with adhesive tape to make a well of 2.5 cm x 1mm thick." PotatoMASH library construction by Maria de la O MOLP Leyva Perez Elution Buffer EB from QIAquick PCR Purification Kit tend to have a lower density than the TBE buffer in which the agarose gel is plunged for the electrophoresis. To avoid loosing the purified library in the buffer it is highly recommended to increase the volume of gel loading buffer compared to a standard electrophoresis. Before to load the libraries in the gel, prepare loading mix composed of 70% Purified library and 30% loading buffer. Mix thoroughly by pipetting, centrifuge in a bench centrifuge for 00:00:30 and load the mix into the wide wells.
Perform the migration at 85 V during 03:00:00 to clearly separate DNA fragments from different sizes.

Place the gel on a transilluminator UV (recommended to use low intensity like 70%) to visualise the DNA bands and excise slices between 270 and 300 bp using a scalpel and place each slice in a 2 mL tube. Gel slices can be stored overnight at low temperature 4 °C (DO NOT freeze the gel slices). However it is recomanded to perform the extraction of the DNA from the gel slices strait after the exicion.

Remove the gel from the migration tank and simmer in TBE buffer containing some GelRed® Nucleic Acid Stain 10000X Water Merck Millipore (EMD Millipore) Catalog #SCT123 , placed on an orbital shaker, agitation about 40 - 45 rpm, 01:00:00
Gel extraction and clean up: This step is performed using the Wizard ‱ SV Gel and PCR Clean-Up System
Last PCR purification using Agencourt AMPure XP magnetic beads:
Weigh each gel slice and add 10 µL of Membrane Binding Solution per 10 mg of gel slice. Vortex and incubate at 50-65 °C until gel slice is completely dissolved.

Insert SV Minicolumn into Collection Tube and Transfer dissolved gel mixture to the Minicolumn assembly. Incubate Room temperature 00:01:00 . Centrifuge 16000 x g, °C, 1 minute for 1 minute. Discard flowthrough and reinsert Minicolumn into Collection Tube

Add 700 µL of Membrane Wash Solution ( ethanol added ). Centrifuge at 16000 x g, °C, 1 minute . Discard flowthrough and reinsert Minicolumn into the collection Tube. Repeat this with 500 µL of Membrane Wash Solution. Centrifuge at 16000 x g, °C, 5 minutes . Empty the Collection Tube and recentrifuge the column assembly 16000 x g, °C, 1 minute with the microcentrifuge lid open (or off) to allow evaporation of any residual ethanol

Carefully transfer Minicolumn to a new 1.5ml microcentrifuge tube and add 50 µL of Nuclease-Free Water to the Minicolumn. Incubate Room temperature 00:01:00 . Centrifuge at 16000 x g, °C, 1 minute . Discard Minicolumn and store DNA at 4 °C for very short storage (ex. overnight) or -20 °C for a few days.

Mix 1.8 volumes of the AMPure XP magnetic bead solution with 1 volume of purified library into a new nuclease free 1.5 mL tubes. Mix thoroughly by pipetting carefully up and down about 20 times. Be careful not to pipet too fast or abruptly to avoid damaging the DNA fragments (the longest the fragments, the more fragile they are). Incubate Room temperature 00:05:00 .
Place the tube on a magnetic rack, compatible with 1.5 mL tubes and wait for the beads to bind to the wall of the tube (magnet side). The solution must be clear before to perform the next steps.
Repeat step 30.4 and let the bead aggregate to dry for a couple of minutes with the lid open.
Immerge the beads into 70% ethanol and incubate Room temperature 00:00:30 . Remove the ethanol by pipetting, without disturbing the beads.

Remove the cleared solution by pipetting. Do not disturb the beads aggregate or aspirate some beads. If beads are pipetted up, put the solution back in the tube, wait for the solution to clear again and restart pipetting. The tube stays on the magnetic rack.
Replace the tube on the magnetic rack and wait for the beads to aggregate again on the tube's wall.

Before the bead aggregate to dry completely and appear cracked, remove the tube from the magnetic rack and add 40 µL of ddH 2 O to perform the elution. Carefully mix by pipetting up and down about 20 times the beads into the water. incubate Room temperature 00:02:00 .

Carefully pipette up the cleared water containing the purified DNA and place it into a new nuclease free tube. Do not pipet any beads, it is preferable to leave a few microliters of water with the beads instead of including beads in the final eluted product. The final library can now be frozen at -20 °C or -80 °C depending on the use and be sent for sequencing

It is recommended to check the size of the DNA fragments included in the final library, as well as the quantity of DNA. Make a 2% agarose gel and migrate 2 µL of the final library in the TBE agarose gel at 85 V during 02:00:00 . Proceed as in go to step #28.3 and go to step #28.4 to observe the bands corresponding to the DNA fragments.

Asses the DNA concentration of the final library using: Qubit fluorometer (designed to measure DNA, RNA, and protein quantity or quality using the Invitrogen Qubit RNA IQ (integrity & quality) assay. , ThermoFisher, Q33238) Broad range assay. Use a volume of 2 µL of the final library to be measured.
